description_long: This object is a stylized representation of a space shuttle, featuring a sharply pointed nose, a black underbelly, and a white upper fuselage. The wings are short and triangular, extending from the lower sides of the fuselage, with a slight upward angle. The tail section is prominent, with a tall vertical stabilizer and two smaller horizontal stabilizers at the base. The surface is smooth and appears to be constructed from a combination of lightweight composite materials and metal, typical of aerospace vehicles. The cockpit area is located at the front, with a slightly raised canopy, and the body is segmented with visible panel lines. No visible engines or landing gear are present in the images, suggesting a simplified or stylized model.
description: A stylized space shuttle with a pointed nose, black underbelly, white fuselage, and short triangular wings. Features a tall vertical tail and a smooth, segmented surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, wide triangular wings, and the black underbelly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tall vertical stabilizer, segmented fuselage, and the thickness of the wings.
description_view_2: Top view displaying the full wingspan, cockpit placement, and the overall shuttle shape.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the vertical stabilizer and wing profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with short, straight wings and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No visible engines present on the exterior.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single tall vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers at the base.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a slightly raised canopy at the front of the fuselage.
